codeReviewIssues.description: single_page_vma test comment says 'Smallest valid VMA: one byte' with range (0x1000, 0x1001). In kernel context VMAs are page-aligned, so the smallest real VMA is one page. The test correctly exercises the 1-byte range logic, but the comment is slightly misleading about what 'smallest valid' means in practice. No code change needed.

summary: Clean pass. The audit of memory_region.rs (0 unsafe blocks, pure logic) is thorough and correct. The AUDIT marker accurately documents findings: binary search insert/lookup/remove verified for empty list, adjacent VMAs, gaps, boundary addresses, zero-length and inverted ranges. The claim of '3 call sites enforcing no-overlaps invariant' is verified correct (address_space.rs:501, process.rs:128, process.rs:169). Eight new edge case tests are well-designed: zero-length VMA, inverted range, duplicate start, reinsert after remove, zero address, remove first/last, and single-page VMA. Combined with the pre-existing 21 tests in vma.rs and 7 adversarial tests in adversarial_vma.rs, coverage is comprehensive. No bugs found — valid outcome for this simple pure-logic module. Worker followed the kernel-auditor skill procedure faithfully with no deviations. Handoff verification shows 974 tests passing and kernel build clean.
